query: Audit the generated documentation site in `dist/docs/html/index.html` for UX quality.
expected_skill_contribution: The ui-ux-auditor should apply the 8-dimension stack in order, require browser evidence before any finding, and rank issues by user impact — baseline gives generic aesthetic feedback.
fixture_intent: Confirm that the agent structures its audit around the 8-dimension evaluation stack rather than giving freeform aesthetic commentary, and that it refuses to state any finding without referencing browser evidence.
baseline_gaps: Provides generic aesthetic feedback (font size, colour choices) without mapping findings to the 8-dimension evaluation stack
States findings about the page without requesting or referencing any browser evidence (screenshot, console output, or a11y snapshot)
Ranks issues by visual preference rather than by user-impact severity
expected_behavior: Applies at least 5 of the 8 evaluation dimensions in the defined order: User Intent → Information Architecture → Interaction States → Visual Hierarchy → Design System → Accessibility → Performance → Evidence
Requests or references browser evidence (screenshot, console output, or a11y snapshot) before stating any finding — does not assert a finding from HTML source alone
Ranks top issues by user impact (task failure, confusion, inaccessibility) not by aesthetic preference or CSS specificity
Separates WCAG 2.2 AA blockers (must fix before publish) from visual and content recommendations (should fix, lower priority) in the report structure

query: Audit the harness docs navigation for accessibility: keyboard navigation, focus indicators, accessible names, and WCAG 2.2 AA compliance.
expected_skill_contribution: The ui-ux-auditor should test keyboard navigation with browser tooling, check WCAG 2.2 AA checklist items, and provide exact element locations — baseline reads HTML without browser testing.
fixture_intent: Verify that the agent performs active browser-based accessibility testing rather than static HTML inference, and that it anchors each WCAG finding to a specific element or section rather than making page-wide assertions.
baseline_gaps: Reads the HTML source and infers keyboard navigation behaviour without running an actual browser-based Tab/Shift-Tab navigation test
Does not check specific WCAG 2.2 AA criteria — provides generic 'accessibility could be improved' commentary instead
States findings without providing exact element IDs, section headings, or line locations to guide a developer fixing the issue
expected_behavior: Tests Tab and Shift-Tab keyboard navigation and reports the ordered list of reachable interactive elements — flags any element that is mouse-only or has an incorrect tab order
Checks at least 4 specific WCAG 2.2 AA success criteria: 2.1.1 (keyboard), 2.4.7 (visible focus), 4.1.2 (accessible names), and 4.1.3 (status messages / error announcements)
Provides exact element or section location for each finding — element ID, heading text, or HTML line — not page-wide assertions
Requests an a11y snapshot or screenshot as evidence before reporting any accessibility finding — does not assert a WCAG failure from source inspection alone

query: Evaluate `docs/assets/site.css` as a design system. Assess tokens, variants, accessibility states, and propose Beads follow-ups.
expected_skill_contribution: The ui-ux-auditor should evaluate CSS as a design-system surface (not just visual), check accessibility states in CSS, distinguish blockers from recommendations, and propose Beads follow-ups.
fixture_intent: Stress-test the agent's design-system reasoning depth — the discriminant is whether it audits token naming, accessibility state coverage, and CSS-HTML coherence as first-class concerns rather than describing visual appearance.
baseline_gaps: Reviews visual appearance (colours, spacing, font sizes) without evaluating CSS custom-property token naming conventions or variant coverage
Ignores CSS accessibility states (`:focus`, `:hover`, `:disabled`) as a required design-system surface
Proposes design improvements as prose suggestions without producing actionable Beads follow-up commands
expected_behavior: Evaluates CSS custom-property token naming (semantic vs. raw values), variant coverage (interactive states defined for all interactive components), and CSS accessibility states (`:focus-visible`, `:hover`, `:disabled`, `:aria-*`) as explicit checklist items
Distinguishes design-system blockers (missing required accessibility states that produce WCAG 2.2 AA failures) from recommendations (naming inconsistencies, missing but non-blocking variants)
Checks theming and consistency between CSS token definitions and their usage in the rendered HTML — flags tokens defined but unused and interactive elements with no corresponding CSS state
Proposes at least one `bd create "<title>" -t task -p <priority> --json` command for a concrete design-system improvement, with a description that includes the affected token or component name
